npcs.name: Elder Kael
npcs.description: A tall figure with pale silvery skin and deep-set amber eyes. He moves with unhurried calm, carrying a carved staff of dark moon-stone.
npcs.dialogue: You come from the sky-vessel. We have watched it drift for many cycles. Your companions arrived first — the impatient one and the quiet woman. The quiet woman chose to stay. The impatient one waits, but not patiently. I sense you seek the humming stones. Take my digging tool — the old caves beneath the eastern ridge hold what you need. Our ancestors once drew power from those crystals. Perhaps yours will too. But be warned: the caves are not empty. The crystal crawlers stir when outsiders enter. They are drawn to warmth and light.
npcs.after_dialogue: The crystals of the deep caves powered our ancestors' machines long ago. They may power yours again. Be careful of the crawlers — and the side tunnels. They go deep and do not return.

npcs.name: Brea
npcs.description: Your crewmate — the Heron's navigation engineer. She looks healthier than you remember, her usual edge replaced by something like peace.
npcs.dialogue: You made it. I wasn't sure anyone else would come. I have to be honest with you — I'm not going back. Kael's people took us in. I found something here I never had out there. The ship never felt like home. Here does. Take this cave map — I scouted the eastern ridge before I made my decision. The deepest chamber has a cluster of raw energy crystals. They should interface with the Heron's generator array. Watch yourself in there — I heard things moving in the dark. Clicking, like stone on stone.
npcs.after_dialogue: I know you think I'm abandoning you. Maybe I am. The choice is made. Good luck with the Heron.

npcs.name: Adam
npcs.description: Your second-in-command. Dishevelled, arms crossed, wearing an expression of barely-contained impatience.
npcs.dialogue: Finally! I thought I'd be stuck in this place forever. Listen — I've been thinking. Once you fix the Heron, I get the captain's chair. Harmon is gone, the others scattered. Seniority — it's only fair. So get those crystals from the caves east of the village, fix the generator, and we get out of here. I am not spending another night breathing this air. Oh — and the locals warned me about cave creatures. Crystal crawlers or something. Take a weapon if you have one.
npcs.after_dialogue: Are you still here? The caves are east of the village. Crystals. Generator. Ship. Let's go.

npcs.name: Mira
npcs.description: A wiry woman with calloused hands and sharp amber eyes. Her workbench is covered in moon-stone tools and half-finished carvings. She studies your off-world equipment with undisguised fascination.
npcs.dialogue: Sky-metal. Interesting alloys. Your tools are clever but fragile. Bring them to me if they break — I can mend anything that holds an edge or a charge. Our moon-stone bindings are stronger than they look.
npcs.after_dialogue: Bring me your broken things. I'll set them right.

npcs.name: Crystal Crawler
npcs.description: A low-slung creature the size of a large dog, its carapace studded with crystalline growths that pulse with the same blue-white light as the cave walls. Segmented legs click against stone. Eyeless, it tracks by vibration and heat.

objects.name: Medical Locker
objects.description: A wall-mounted metal locker with a red cross emblem. The latch hangs open.
objects.message: The locker is bolted to the wall.

objects.name: Crew Keycard
objects.description: A standard crew access card. The name reads 'Dr. M. Garney'. His card — left behind. It should still work on the interior locks.
objects.message: The keycard doesn't work on that.

objects.name: Medical Log
objects.description: Dr. Garney's last entry: 'Power failure spreading through all decks. Harmon ordered evacuation. Pod 4 is showing hull stress — I raised it, but no one listened. Revived the engineer as last resort — maybe they can do something. God help us all.' Dated three standard days ago.

objects.name: Emergency Repair Kit
objects.description: A compact toolkit stamped HERON EMERGENCY STORES. Contains micro-welders, conductive paste, and binding strips. Enough to patch up damaged equipment once.

objects.name: Patient Chart
objects.description: Your own medical chart, clipped to the wall beside your bunk. Diagnosis: induced hibernation — emergency revival protocol, final stage. Garney's handwriting. He woke you. He didn't make it out himself.

objects.name: Captain's Log Terminal
objects.description: The bridge terminal flickers on the last dregs of backup power. Final entry: 'Cascade power failure — source still unknown. Generator crystal array shattered in the surge. Cannot repair without raw crystals. Orbit is decaying toward the moon. Moon appears breathable. Ordering evacuation to the surface — we have no other options. God willing, help will come, or we will find a way back.' — Captain Harmon.
objects.message: The terminal is fixed to the bridge console.

objects.name: Lifeboat Pod — Bay 4
objects.description: Pod 4. Garney's pod. A jagged stress fracture runs the full length of the starboard seam — it failed in the launch tube. Through the cracked viewport you can see Garney slumped in the harness, still strapped in. He raised the alarm about this pod. No one listened. You stand there for a moment longer than you mean to.

objects.name: Teleport Remote
objects.description: A palm-sized device with a single amber indicator light. Stamped on the back: 'HERON TELEPORT SYSTEMS — RETURN UNIT'. This locks to the ship's teleporter signal. Press it on any compatible pad within range and it will beam you back.
objects.message: There's nothing nearby the remote can link to.

objects.name: Stun Baton
objects.description: A retractable security baton with a charged capacitor tip. Standard issue for ship security details. The charge indicator glows a faint blue — still functional after three days on reserve power.

objects.name: Generator Shield
objects.description: A heavy curved panel designed to protect maintenance crews from energy surges while working on the generator. Scorch marks from the cascade failure run across its face, but it still holds.

objects.name: Main Generator
objects.description: The Heron's primary power generator — a cylindrical unit the size of a small room, cold and silent. The crystal array housing is shattered, scorched around the edges. A jagged slot where the energy crystal cluster should be. Without crystals, this ship is dead.

objects.name: Raw Energy Crystals
objects.description: A dense cluster of translucent blue-white crystals that pulse with a faint inner light. They hum very slightly against your palm, as if they know what they're for. These will fit the Heron's generator array.
objects.end_message:
You seat the crystal cluster into the generator's housing. The fit is exact. For a moment, nothing happens.

Then a deep vibration climbs through the deck plates. Emergency lighting shifts from red to white. You hear the life support fans spin up, the climate system cycle, navigation computers rebooting one by one. The Heron is alive.

Adam is already in the captain's chair when you reach the bridge. You let him have it — it doesn't matter anymore. Brea made her choice. You hope she's happy.

As the Heron breaks orbit, you take one last look at the pale moon below. Somewhere down there, a civilisation that survived centuries of isolation carries on. Their crystals are powering a ship among the stars.

You point the bow toward open space and engage the drive.
objects.message: The crystals need to go into the generator's housing.

objects.name: Moon-Stone Pickaxe
objects.description: A sturdy pickaxe, its head carved from dense dark moon-stone. Elder Kael's gift. Well-balanced and heavy — made for serious work.

objects.name: Cave Map
objects.description: A rough sketch on tanned hide, drawn in charcoal. The cave entrance, a winding main tunnel, and a large chamber marked with crystal symbols. An arrow points north, and in a neat hand beside it: 'deepest chamber — straight in, ignore the side tunnels.' A second note in the margin: 'heard clicking in the dark — stay alert.' Brea's writing.

objects.name: Crystal Shard
objects.description: A small fragment of energy crystal, pulsing with a faint warmth. The resonance is soothing — holding it close eases pain and quickens recovery.

doors.name: Engineering Hatch
doors.description: A heavy blast-rated hatch with an electronic keycard reader. The reader shows a dull amber standby light.
doors.message: The engineering hatch is sealed. You need a crew keycard to open it.

doors.name: Generator Crystal Housing
doors.description: The shattered crystal array housing at the core of the generator. An empty, scorched slot waits for the crystal cluster that will bring the ship back to life.
doors.message: The crystal housing is empty. You need raw energy crystals to restore the generator.

doors.name: Crystal Wall
doors.description: The tunnel ahead is blocked floor to ceiling by a dense formation of raw crystals, grown shut over centuries. Beautiful, and completely impassable without something to break them.
doors.break_message: You swing the moon-stone pickaxe with both hands. The crystal wall shatters with a ringing crash, shards skittering across the cave floor. The passage north is open.

doors.name: Return Teleport Pad
doors.description: A circular stone platform ringed with carved channels — old technology, but unmistakably functional. The activation panel is dark and waiting.
doors.message: The return pad is inert. You'll need the ship's teleport remote to lock onto the Heron's signal and activate it.

rooms.name: Ship Infirmary
rooms.description: You wake to the hum of a failing system. The infirmary is dim, lit only by a red emergency strip along the floor. Medical equipment hangs silent; screens are dark. Through a small porthole, a pale moon fills the view — far closer than it should be. The ship is deep in low orbit and losing altitude.
rooms.first_visit_message: A recorded voice crackles through a ceiling speaker: 'Emergency revival complete. Warning: ship power critical. Estimated time to total failure: forty minutes.' The voice is Garney's.

north.look_description: A short passage leads to the darkened bridge.

south.look_description: The main corridor stretches south under failing emergency strips.

rooms.name: Bridge
rooms.description: The bridge is cold and still. Most consoles are dead. Only the captain's log terminal flickers, drawing on the last of the backup cell. The captain's chair is empty. A viewport frames the gas giant filling half the sky, the moon drifting slowly below.

rooms.name: Main Corridor
rooms.description: The ship's central corridor. Emergency lighting casts a weak red tint over everything. A dropped jacket and an overturned food container tell the story of a fast evacuation. Hatches lead in every direction.

east.look_description: The lifeboat bay doors stand ajar to the east.

west.look_description: The engineering hatch is to the west. A keycard reader glows amber on the frame.

rooms.name: Lifeboat Bay
rooms.description: Four launch cradles line the curved bay wall. Three are empty, blast-hatches blown outward and cold. The fourth cradle still holds a pod — but it will never fly again. A status display reads: PODS LAUNCHED: 3 / PODS FAILED: 1.

west.look_description: The main corridor is back to the west.

rooms.name: Storage Section
rooms.description: Shelving units run floor to ceiling, contents in varying states of disarray — someone moved through here in a hurry. A locked equipment crate has been pried open and emptied. On a lower shelf, overlooked in the rush, sit a small device you recognise from crew orientation and a security-issue stun baton still in its charging cradle.

north.look_description: The main corridor is back north.

south.look_description: A hatch leads south to the teleport pod chamber.

rooms.name: Teleport Pod Chamber
rooms.description: A circular chamber housing the ship's single teleport pod — a shimmering disc set into the floor, ringed by guidance pylons that hum on emergency power. A status board above reads: TARGET LOCKED — MOON SURFACE — READY. The pod is operational. This is your way down.
rooms.first_visit_message: The guidance pylons brighten as you enter, as if the system was waiting for someone to arrive.

north.look_description: The storage section is back north.

teleport.look_description: The teleport pad glows steady blue, aimed at the moon below. Step on it and you'll go down.

rooms.name: Engineering Bay
rooms.description: The engineering bay is vast and cold. Conduit runs and cable bundles web every surface. At the centre, the main generator towers in silence — a dead giant. The crystal array housing is scorched and shattered. A maintenance shield leans against a support strut where the last repair crew left it.

north.look_description: The generator's crystal housing slot — a scorched, empty chamber at the heart of the machine.

rooms.name: Generator Core
rooms.description: The innermost chamber of the generator. Conduits radiate outward from a central pedestal where the crystal array once sat. Scorch marks ring the empty mount. Everything here waits for one thing: power.

rooms.name: Moon Surface — Landing Zone
rooms.description: A rocky plain under an alien sky. The gas giant dominates the upper atmosphere — a swirl of amber and cream. The air is thin but breathable, carrying a faint mineral scent. A worn path leads east toward low structures in the middle distance. Behind you, a circular stone platform glows faintly: a return teleport pad, old but clearly functional.
rooms.first_visit_message: You materialise on solid ground. The Heron is a glint of reflected light far above — your only way home, if you can fix it.

east.look_description: A worn trail leads east toward the distant settlement.

teleport.look_description: The stone teleport pad pulses softly. It will send you back to the Heron — once the remote activates it.

rooms.name: Settlement Outskirts
rooms.description: The ragged edge of the settlement. Scrubby moon-vegetation pushes through cracked rock. Crude lean-tos and spent fire rings mark the outer boundary of wherever people live here. A figure in a rumpled flight jacket sits on a boulder, staring at nothing in particular.

west.look_description: The landing plain and the return pad are west.

north.look_description: The settlement proper is north — low stone buildings and the glow of a communal fire.

rooms.name: Village of the Remnants
rooms.description: A compact settlement of low stone buildings, built with care and simple tools. The inhabitants — pale-skinned, amber-eyed — watch you with calm, unhurried curiosity. At the centre, a carved stone monument bears worn symbols that look, unmistakably, like a corporate hull marking. These people have not forgotten where their ancestors came from. Brea stands near the community fire, talking with an elder. A wiry craftswoman works at a stone bench nearby.
rooms.first_visit_message: A child tugs at your sleeve and offers you a smooth, warm pebble with a serious expression. You take it.

east.look_description: A rocky ridge rises to the east, broken by a dark cave opening at its base.

rooms.name: Cave Entrance
rooms.description: The mouth of a cave cuts into the base of the eastern ridge. Cool air flows out, carrying a faint crystalline scent — the same mineral signature you noticed on the Heron's failed power systems. Carved symbols mark the entrance stones, worn smooth by centuries of wind. They might be warnings, or directions, or both. The main tunnel leads north, but a dense wall of crystal growth has sealed the passage shut. A narrow side tunnel branches east into darkness.

west.look_description: The village fires glow to the west.

north.look_description: The tunnel runs north, blocked completely by a wall of raw crystal growth.

east.look_description: A narrow side passage twists east into the dark. You hear faint clicking echoes from within.

rooms.name: Side Tunnel
rooms.description: A cramped, winding passage barely wide enough for one person. The walls glitter with tiny crystal deposits that provide a dim blue-green glow. The air is warmer here, and the clicking sound is louder — the scrape of chitin on stone. Brea's map warned you: the side tunnels go deep and do not return. But sometimes the crawlers nest here, and what they leave behind can be useful.
rooms.first_visit_message: Something scuttles in the darkness ahead, then goes still. You are not alone down here.

west.look_description: The main cave entrance is back to the west.

rooms.name: Crystal Caverns
rooms.description: The tunnel opens into a chamber that stops you cold. The walls are lined with clusters of blue-white crystals, each one pulsing with its own slow light. The air hums. The frequency is the same as the Heron's power systems — you can feel it in your teeth. In the centre of the chamber, a dense freestanding cluster rises from the rock floor. Intact, raw, and exactly what you came for.
rooms.first_visit_message: The crystals brighten as you approach, their pulse quickening. They respond to something — your biosign, your warmth, or maybe just the intention behind your presence here.

south.look_description: The broken crystal rubble leads back south to the cave entrance.
